LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT, THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION: DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE MOTION IS NOT PRECLUDED BY THE SCO'S PRIOR DEMURRER, IT IS DENIED BECAUSE THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS STATE A CLAIM AGAINST THE SCO BASED ON VICARIOUS LIABILITY FOR ABUSE OF PROCESS COMMITTED BY THE SCO'S EMPLOYEES AND AGENTS PER GOVERNMENT CODE 815.2 AND 815.4. UNLIKE MIKLOSY V. REGENTS (2008) 44 CAL. 4TH 876, WHICH INVOLVED THE TORT OF WRONGFUL TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT WHICH COULD ONLY BE COMMITTED BY AN EMPLOYER, THE TORT OF ABUSE OF PROCESS CAN BE COMMITTED BY AN EMPLOYEE OR AGENT. ALTHOUGH ONLY THE SCO, NOT ITS EMPLOYEES OR AGENTS, CAN PERFORM AN UNCLAIMED PROPERTY AUDIT AND ONLY THE SCO, NOT ITS EMPLOYEES OR AGENTS, WAS A PARTY TO THE PRIOR LAWSUIT, NEITHER OF THOSE FACTS NECESSARILY PRECLUDE THE SCO'S AGENTS AND EMPLOYEES FROM MISUSING DOCUMENTS PRODUCED IN THE PRIOR LAWSUIT IN A MANNER THAT COULD IMPOSE ABUSE OF PROCESS LIABILITY ON THEM PERSONALLY AND THE SCO VICARIOUSLY. AS FAR AS THE COURT IS AWARE, NO CASE HAS EXTENDED THE HOLDING OR REASONING OF MIKLOSY TO A TORT SUCH AS ABUSE OF PROCESS THAT CAN BE COMMITTED BY AN EMPLOYEE OR AGENT. THE COURT ORDERS THIS ACTION STAYED, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF MR. COHEN'S DIRECT TESTIMONY THAT WAS OTHERWISE REQUIRED BY A PRIOR ORDER, IF THE CONTROLLER FILES A WRIT BY THE END OF THE MONTH. THE STAY WILL LAST UNTIL THE CALIFORNIA COURT OF APPEAL EITHER DENIES THE WRIT OR RULES OTHERWISE. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew B. Kay of the law offices of Cozen O'Connor (202-912-4864) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Andrew D. Lanphere of the law offices of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (415-983-1321) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorneys, Marc S. Cohen and Steven S. Rosenthal of the law offices of Loeb & Loeb LLP (310-282-2337) appear as counsels for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT, THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED. Although the motion is not precluded by the SCO's prior demurrer, it is denied because the allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans state a claim against the SCO based on vicarious liability for abuse of process committed by the SCO's employees and agents per Government Code 815.2 and 815.4. Unlike Miklosy v. Regents (2008) 44 Cal. 4th 876, which involved the tort of wrongful termination of employment which could only be committed by an employer, the tort of abuse of process can be committed by an employee or agent. Although only the SCO, not its employees or agents, can perform an unclaimed property audit and only the SCO, not its employees or agents, was a party to the prior lawsuit, neither of those facts necessarily preclude the SCO's agents and employees from misusing documents produced in the prior lawsuit in a manner that could impose abuse of process liability on them personally and the SCO vicariously. As far as the court is aware, no case has extended the holding or reasoning of Miklosy to a tort such as abuse of process that can be committed by an employee or agent. The Court orders this action stayed, with the exception of Mr. Cohen's direct testimony that was otherwise required by a prior order, if the Controller files a writ by the end of the month. The stay will last until the California Court of Appeal either denies the writ or rules otherwise. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, e-mail: ssawyer@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew D. Lanphere of the law offices of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (415.983.1321) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party, Thrivent Financial for Lutherans

Attorney, Andrew B. Kay of the law offices of Cozen O'Connor (202.912.4864) appears as counsel Pro Hac Vice for plaintiff/moving party, Thrivent Financial for Lutherans

Attorneys, Steven S. Rosenthal, Alicia M. Clough and Marc S. Cohen, each of the law offices of Loeb & Loeb LLP (310.282.2337) appear as counsel for defendant/opposing party, California State Controller

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests And To Compel Production Of Documents Withheld Based On Privilege is DENIED in substantial part. The documents listed on the Controller's privilege and redaction log are protected from disclosure by either the work product privilege and/or the attorney-client privilege. Communications between counsel and an undisclosed expert are protected from disclosure by the work product doctrine. Communications between and among counsel for the same client pertaining to litigation are protected from disclosure by the attorney-client privilege. Thrivent has not shown that the Controller has waived either the right to assert the work product doctrine or the attorney-client privilege by either disclosure of a significant part of the communications or by putting the communications at issue. The Controller must serve verified amended responses stating that it has produced all responsive documents except those listed on its privilege and redaction log. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Kay of Cozen O'Connor telephone number 202-912-4864 for Plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans.
Andrew Lanphere of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP telephone number 415-983-1321 for Plaintiff Thrivent Financial for Lutherans.
Steven Rosenthal of Loeb & Loeb LLP telephone number 202-618-5000 for Defendant Office of the State Controller.
Alicia Clough and Marc Cohen of Loeb & Loeb LLP telephone number 310-282-2000 for Defendants Betty Yee and Office of the State Controller.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative.
DEFENDANT T YEE, The Controller's demurrer to the sole cause of action for abuse of process is overruled. Thrivent has adequately alleged that the Controller had an ulterior motive in using the discovery processes and that it committed a willful act by using the discovery it received from Thrivent in violation of court orders. The complaint does not disclose on its face that the abuse of process claim is necessarily barred by either the discretionary immunity or the litigation privilege. There is no discretion to violate a court order and the alleged audit is not necessarily a communication for purposes of Civil Code 47(b). The Controller's request for judicial notice is denied as to exhibit D because the legal effect of that email chain is fairly subject to dispute. The court did not consider the Controller's argument made for the first time in the reply brief that Thrivent has not adequately pled a legal injury because Thrivent did not have an opportunity to respond in writing to that argument. Defendant has 30 days to answer. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
